<<<KOT:supinely>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"supinely is an adverbial manner modifier describing body orientation (head facing up). Its core sense requires a specific spatial-anatomical relation (the head's position relative to the rest of the body and gravity) that cannot be adequately expressed using the prime lexicon. The concept of 'facing up' or 'head upward orientation' requires technical spatial primitives not available in the allowed lexicon."}
<<<END:supinely>>>>

<<<KOT:taconite>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"taconite is a technical mineralogical and geological kind defined by its specific composition (banded iron formation with iron minerals interlayered with quartz and chert) and geological provenance (Precambrian Biwabik Iron Formation). Its identity conditions require technical chemistry and geology (iron oxide percentages, mineral phases, stratigraphic context) that lie outside the scope of the everyday prime lexicon and cannot be grounded in the available catalog."}
<<<END:taconite>>>>

<<<KOT:tendencious>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"tendencious is marked as an obsolete spelling variant of tendentious in the source.
